vectors.name: Int Function
vectors.description: Returns the integer part of a real number (as a Real).

quirks_and_tests.description: Unlike QBasic's INT, Pascal's Int simply truncates towards zero (like QBasic's FIX), and it returns a Real type, not an Integer.

vectors.name: Trunc Function
vectors.description: Truncates a real number to an integer value.

quirks_and_tests.description: This is the exact integer equivalent of QBasic's FIX. It truncates towards zero.

vectors.name: Round Function
vectors.description: Rounds a real-type value to the nearest integer. If the value is exactly halfway between two integers, it rounds to the number with the greatest absolute magnitude (away from zero).

quirks_and_tests.description: Turbo Pascal employs standard arithmetic rounding (rounding away from zero). A fractional part of exactly .5 will always be rounded to the number with the greatest absolute magnitude, meaning positive numbers round up and negative numbers round down.

vectors.name: Div Operator (Integer Division)
vectors.description: Divides two integers and returns the integer quotient.

quirks_and_tests.description: Unlike QBasic's backslash (\), Pascal's Div operator only accepts Integer types. It does not perform pre-rounding on Reals. It simply truncates the quotient.

vectors.name: Mod Operator (Modulo)
vectors.description: Returns the remainder of integer division.

quirks_and_tests.description: Like Div, Mod only operates on Integer types in Turbo Pascal. The sign of the result is always the sign of the first operand.
